summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of a circle?

Definitions A circle is the set of all points in a plane equidistant from a given point called the center. The radius (r) is the distance from the center to any point on the circle. The other options — 'A polygon with infinitely many sides', 'A curved line that encloses a region', and 'A shape whose area equals Ï€d' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

If a circle has a radius of 9 cm, what is its diameter?

The radius (r) is the distance from the center to any point on the circle. The other options — '9 cm', '4.5 cm', and '27 cm' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the circumference of a circle with a diameter of 14 cm? (Use Ï€ â‰ˆ 3.14)

The circumference of a circle is C = 2πr (or πd). Substitute the radius or diameter and calculate. The other options — '87.92 cm', '21.98 cm', and '153.86 cm' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
